description_long: This is a large, double-edged battle axe with a robust, cylindrical handle and a dramatic, asymmetrical blade profile. The blade features a broad, sweeping curve on one side and a sharply pointed tip on the other, giving it a menacing and functional appearance. The handle appears to be reinforced with metal bands and decorative elements, ending in a pointed pommel. The axe head is mounted securely atop the handle, with a central spike extending upward, suggesting both offensive and defensive capabilities. The entire object is rendered in a dark, metallic color, giving it a heavy, formidable look. There are no visible engravings or markings on the surface.
description: A large, double-edged battle axe with a dark, metallic finish, featuring a sweeping curved blade and a reinforced cylindrical handle.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the front, showing the full width of the asymmetrical blade and the central spike above the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, revealing the thin profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the opposite side of the blade, mirroring the front but with the blade's curve facing the other direction.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the other side, again showing the thin blade profile and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight handle, likely wood with metal reinforcement, textured for grip, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Asymmetrical, double-edged blade with a sweeping curve on one side, pointed tip on the other, and a central upward spike.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible reinforcement at the head-handle junction.
